title: The Limestone Shelf

features.name: shelf edge
features.desc-on-touch: Where the river narrows and the limestone rises. From here the water sees its own flow for the first time. The curve teaches it comparison.

features.name: erosion circuit
features.desc-on-touch: Limestone surface etched with flow-patterns. Water has written its own path-memory into stone. Each groove is an archived moment of flowing.

features.name: water witness
features.desc-on-touch: Silicon fragment embedded in stone, recording temperature and volume across centuries. Water learns its own history by reading this witness.

features.name: pool of reflection
features.desc-on-touch: Where the river pools before the drop. In still water, the current sees its own reflection and forgets which one is moving downstream.

features.name: limestone ledger
features.desc-on-touch: Survey marks carved decades ago show the shelf's descent. New marks continue them downward. The stone is recording its own wearing away.

inscription: the water learns its flow by learning the stone; the stone learns itself by learning the water's thought

file.name: Limestone_Shelf_Water_Comparison_1987-Now.mp3
file.note: Recordings of water-sound before and after shelf seasons. The recordings have begun to converge, as if the river compares itself across time.

exits.label: where the water cascades to meet itself
down where the limestone deepens
exits.to: NEW: the submerged shelf where the river-question continues
